title: Guizotia jacksonii
intro: Guizotia jacksonii, also known as Wild dill or African dill, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, and has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Guizotia jacksonii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Guizotia jacksonii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Guizotia jacksonii is classified under the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. The family Asteraceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Guizotia jacksonii belonging to the subfamily Lactucoideae. The genus Guizotia is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species, which is Guizotia jacksonii. Guizotia jacksonii is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Africa.
